## [0.2.0](https://github.com/awinogradov/code-assistants/compare/code-review-action@v0.1.0...code-review-action@v0.2.0) (2026-05-29)

## Release Notes

The code review action now handles rapid-fire comments properly and knows when to hold back on routine acknowledgements.

## ✨ What's New

### Smarter comment handling
The AI code reviewer now gracefully handles comment bursts on pull requests. When multiple comments mentioning the bot arrive in quick succession, each one gets properly addressed instead of some being silently dropped due to workflow cancellations. Your reviewer stays responsive even during active discussions.

<details><summary>Related issues</summary>

- [#71: Code review action drops bot mentions when comments arrive in quick succession](https://github.com/awinogradov/code-assistants/issues/71)
</details>

### Quieter acknowledgement responses
Your AI reviewer now recognizes when you're simply acknowledging its feedback (like replying "Fixed" or "Done") and responds with a simple 👍 reaction instead of generating another comment. It still provides full responses when you ask questions or request re-reviews, keeping the conversation focused and reducing notification noise.

<details><summary>Related issues</summary>

- [#111: Code review react mode replies to every review-thread acknowledgement](https://github.com/awinogradov/code-assistants/issues/111)
</details>

### Automated release merging
Release pull requests that are approved and pass all checks now merge automatically. This eliminates the manual merge step between approval and publish, streamlining your release process. The action integrates with your existing release workflows and syncs to any downstream repositories you've configured.

<details><summary>Related issues</summary>

- [#107: Add release-automerge composite action with downstream sync workflow](https://github.com/awinogradov/code-assistants/issues/107)
</details>

## 🐛 Bug Fixes

### Release PR approval workflow
Release pull requests can now be properly auto-approved by the code review bot. Previously, releases were authored by the same bot account that reviews them, which GitHub blocks (users can't approve their own PRs). The release creation process now uses a distinct author identity, allowing the automated approval flow to work as designed.

## ⚙️ Configuration Required

### Release author token update
To enable proper auto-approval of release PRs, ensure your `release-create.yml` workflow uses a different token for creating releases than your reviewer bot uses. The release PR author and the code review bot must have distinct GitHub identities.

Update your workflow to use a personal token (like `secrets.GH_TOKEN`) instead of the bot token for release creation:
- In `release-create.yml`, the release action should use a token associated with a different user than your `BOT_TOKEN`
- Keep using `BOT_TOKEN` for the code review action itself
- Set `RELEASE_PR_AUTHORS` environment variable to match the username associated with your release creation token
